# In Process

> A timeline for artists. It includes a protocol, an API, and multiple front-end clients including Web, Telegram, and SMS.

In Process is a living archive of artistic evolution where artists, builders, and creatives can upload their work-in-progress and mint it onchain to a collective timeline. Your community, fans, and patrons can collect, comment, and support work as it unfolds.

## What Makes In Process Different

- Focus on process, not polish
- Permanent, onchain documentation
- Monetization of in-progress work
- Decentralized, artist-owned timelines
- Support for both Web2 and Web3 users
- Cross-platform embedding of content (onchain + offchain)

## What Content Can You Post

You can upload any part of your creative process:
- Journals, notes, poetry
- Demos, drafts, audio clips
- Sketches, moodboards, visuals
- Behind-the-scenes footage, vlogs
- External links and embeds (YouTube, NFTs, Instagram, Google Docs, etc.)

## Timeline Features

- Moments can be backdated (e.g., a sketch from 2021)
- Moments can be future-dated (e.g., a concept for a 2026 project)
- Moments can be time-stamped to the present
- Your personal timeline feeds into the Collective Timeline

## The Collective Timeline

A shared feed where everyone's onchain creative process lives side-by-side. It's a decentralized, living archive of artistic culture across disciplines and styles.

## Who Is In Process For

- Artists (music, visual, literary, film)
- Builders and developers
- Web2 creatives transitioning into Web3
- Fans, patrons, and explorers who want to support artistic journeys

## How Monetization Works

- Set a price per moment (in crypto or USD)
- Collectors pay to own and support your process
- Proceeds go directly to your wallet

## Technology Stack

- Base: Fast, affordable L2 blockchain (Chain ID: 8453)
- Zora Protocol: For minting and managing moments (ERC-1155)
- Auth: Privy-backed email OTP login and API key flows are supported
- Arweave: Permanent decentralized storage for media and metadata
- Payments: Crypto payments are supported; card checkout depends on your client-side provider integration

## Documentation

- Main Documentation: https://docs.inprocess.world
- API Reference: https://docs.inprocess.world/api-reference
- FAQ: https://inprocess.world/faq

## Links

- Website: https://inprocess.world
- Documentation: https://docs.inprocess.world
- FAQ: https://inprocess.world/faq

---

## Building a Custom Web Client on In Process

> This section is for developers and artists who want to build their own web client using the In Process API. Follow these steps in order.

The In Process API is a REST API hosted at `https://api.inprocess.world/api`. It handles all blockchain interactions for you — you do not need to write smart contract code. Your client is a UI layer that calls this API.

**Reference implementation:** https://github.com/homebasedotlove/inprocess-client

### Recommended Architecture

Use Next.js with API proxy routes. Your Next.js `/app/api/` routes forward requests to the In Process API and keep your API key secret on the server. The client only talks to your own server.

```
Browser → /app/api/timeline   → https://api.inprocess.world/api/timeline
Browser → /app/api/collect    → https://api.inprocess.world/api/moment/collect
Browser → /app/api/mint       → https://api.inprocess.world/api/moment/create
Browser → /app/api/upload     → Arweave (server-side upload route)
```

Recommended stack: Next.js 15, TanStack Query, arweave-js (for storage), Tailwind CSS.

For wallet/auth UX, choose what fits your audience and product: Privy (email-first onboarding), wagmi + RainbowKit (wallet-native flow), or another provider. In Process APIs are compatible with different auth/connect approaches.

---

### Step 1: Get Your API Key and Collection Address

Before writing any code you need two things:

1. **An API key** — Generated at `https://inprocess.world/manage/api-keys`. Format: `art_sk_...`. Required to collect moments on behalf of users.
2. **A collection address** — Your ERC-1155 contract address on Base. You can create one at `https://inprocess.world/manage` or via `POST /api/collections`. All moments you mint belong to this collection.

Reference: https://docs.inprocess.world/api-reference/introduction

---

### Step 2: Environment Variables

Create a `.env.local` file in your project root:

```bash
# Required: your In Process API key (keep this secret, server-side only)
INPROCESS_API_KEY=art_sk_...

# Required: your collection contract address on Base
NEXT_PUBLIC_PLATFORM_COLLECTION=0x...

# Required: base64-encoded Arweave private key (JWK format) for media uploads
ARWEAVE_KEY=...

# Optional: wallet address to receive referral rewards on mints
NEXT_PUBLIC_CREATE_REFERRAL=0x...
```

To get your `ARWEAVE_KEY`: generate an Arweave wallet, export the JWK JSON, then base64-encode it: `base64 -w 0 wallet.json` (Linux) or `base64 -i wallet.json` (macOS).

Reference: https://docs.inprocess.world/arweave/upload-to-arweave

---

### Step 3: Display the Timeline

The timeline endpoint is **public** — no API key required.

**Next.js API proxy route** (`app/api/timeline/route.ts`):
```typescript
import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server";

const IN_PROCESS_API = "https://api.inprocess.world/api";

export async function GET(req: NextRequest) {
  const { searchParams } = new URL(req.url);
  const page = searchParams.get("page") || "1";
  const limit = searchParams.get("limit") || "20";
  const collection = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_PLATFORM_COLLECTION;

  const params = new URLSearchParams({ page, limit, collection: collection || "" });
  const res = await fetch(`${IN_PROCESS_API}/timeline?${params}`, {
    next: { revalidate: 30 },
  });
  const data = await res.json();
  return NextResponse.json(data);
}
```

**GET `/api/timeline` — Query parameters:**
| Parameter | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `page` | number | Page number (default: 1) |
| `limit` | number | Results per page (default: 20, max: 100) |
| `collection` | string | Filter by collection address |
| `artist` | string | Filter by artist wallet address |
| `content_type` | string | Filter by MIME prefix: `audio`, `image`, `video` |
| `period` | string | `day`, `week`, `month`, or `all` |
| `channel` | string | `web`, `telegram`, `sms`, or `api` |
| `curated` | string | `true` or `false` (default: `true`) |

**Response shape:**
```json
{
  "moments": [
    {
      "address": "0x...",
      "token_id": "1",
      "chain_id": 8453,
      "id": "uuid",
      "uri": "ar://...",
      "protocol": "in_process",
      "creator": { "address": "0x...", "username": "artistname", "hidden": false },
      "created_at": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z",
      "metadata": {
        "name": "Moment title",
        "description": "Description",
        "image": "ar://...",
        "content": { "mime": "image/jpeg", "uri": "ar://..." },
        "animation_url": "ar://..."
      }
    }
  ],
  "pagination": { "page": 1, "limit": 20, "total_pages": 5 }
}
```

Reference: https://docs.inprocess.world/api-reference/timeline

---

### Step 4: Display a Single Moment and Its Price

Use this to show a moment's metadata and current sale configuration before collecting.

**GET `/api/moment`** — Query parameters: `collectionAddress`, `tokenId`, `chainId` (all required).

```typescript
const res = await fetch(
  `https://api.inprocess.world/api/moment?collectionAddress=${address}&tokenId=${tokenId}&chainId=8453`
);
const moment = await res.json();
// moment.saleConfig.pricePerToken — price in wei (string)
// moment.metadata — same shape as timeline metadata above
```

**Sale config shape:**
```json
{
  "saleConfig": {
    "pricePerToken": "1000000000000000",
    "type": "fixedPrice",
    "saleStart": 1700000000,
    "saleEnd": 9999999999,
    "fundsRecipient": "0x..."
  }
}
```

`pricePerToken` is in wei. Divide by `1e18` for ETH display.

Reference: https://docs.inprocess.world/api-reference/moment/get

---

### Step 5: Connect a Wallet

Use RainbowKit — it handles multi-wallet support with minimal setup.

```bash
npm install @rainbow-me/rainbowkit wagmi viem @tanstack/react-query
```

**`lib/wagmi.ts`:**
```typescript
import { getDefaultConfig } from "@rainbow-me/rainbowkit";
import { base } from "wagmi/chains";

export const config = getDefaultConfig({
  appName: "Your App Name",
  projectId: "YOUR_WALLETCONNECT_PROJECT_ID",
  chains: [base],
});
```

**`providers/WagmiProvider.tsx`:**
```typescript
"use client";
import { WagmiProvider } from "wagmi";
import { RainbowKitProvider } from "@rainbow-me/rainbowkit";
import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider } from "@tanstack/react-query";
import { config } from "@/lib/wagmi";
import "@rainbow-me/rainbowkit/styles.css";

const queryClient = new QueryClient();

export function Providers({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <WagmiProvider config={config}>
      <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>
        <RainbowKitProvider>{children}</RainbowKitProvider>
      </QueryClientProvider>
    </WagmiProvider>
  );
}
```

In your layout: wrap with `<Providers>`. Add `<ConnectButton />` from RainbowKit to your nav.

Reference: https://docs.inprocess.world/api-reference/introduction

---

### Step 6: Collect a Moment

Collecting requires your API key (server-side) and a valid moment payload.

**Next.js API proxy route** (`app/api/collect/route.ts`):
```typescript
import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server";

const IN_PROCESS_API = "https://api.inprocess.world/api";

export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {
  const body = await req.json();
  const res = await fetch(`${IN_PROCESS_API}/moment/collect`, {
    method: "POST",
    headers: {
      "Content-Type": "application/json",
      "x-api-key": process.env.INPROCESS_API_KEY!,
    },
    body: JSON.stringify(body),
  });
  const data = await res.json();
  return NextResponse.json(data);
}
```

**Request body:**
```json
{
  "moment": {
    "collectionAddress": "0x...",
    "tokenId": "1",
    "chainId": 8453
  },
  "amount": 1,
  "comment": "Love this work"
}
```

**Response:**
```json
{ "hash": "0x..." }
```

**CollectButton component** (calls your proxy, not the API directly):
```typescript
"use client";
import { useAccount } from "wagmi";

export function CollectButton({ moment }: { moment: any }) {
  const { isConnected } = useAccount();

  const handleCollect = async () => {
    if (!isConnected) return;
    const res = await fetch("/api/collect", {
      method: "POST",
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      body: JSON.stringify({ moment, amount: 1, comment: "" }),
    });
    const { hash } = await res.json();
    console.log("Collected:", hash);
  };

  return (
    <button onClick={handleCollect} disabled={!isConnected}>
      {isConnected ? "Collect" : "Connect wallet to collect"}
    </button>
  );
}
```

Reference: https://docs.inprocess.world/api-reference/moment/collect

---

### Step 7: Upload Media to Arweave

All media (images, videos, audio) must be uploaded to Arweave before minting. The result is an `ar://` URI used in the moment's metadata.

**Next.js API proxy route** (`app/api/upload/route.ts`):
```typescript
import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server";
import Arweave from "arweave";

const arweave = Arweave.init({ host: "arweave.net", port: 443, protocol: "https" });

export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {
  const formData = await req.formData();
  const file = formData.get("file") as File;
  if (!file) return NextResponse.json({ error: "No file" }, { status: 400 });

  const buffer = Buffer.from(await file.arrayBuffer());
  const key = JSON.parse(
    Buffer.from(process.env.ARWEAVE_KEY!, "base64").toString("utf-8")
  );

  const transaction = await arweave.createTransaction({ data: buffer }, key);
  transaction.addTag("Content-Type", file.type);
  await arweave.transactions.sign(transaction, key);

  const uploader = await arweave.transactions.getUploader(transaction);
  while (!uploader.isComplete) {
    await uploader.uploadChunk();
  }

  return NextResponse.json({ uri: `ar://${transaction.id}` });
}
```

The `ar://` URI is permanent and content-addressed. Use it in your metadata JSON.

Reference: https://docs.inprocess.world/arweave/upload-to-arweave

---

### Step 8: Create (Mint) a Moment

After uploading media and metadata to Arweave, call the create endpoint.

**Metadata JSON structure** — upload this as JSON to Arweave first:
```json
{
  "name": "Moment title",
  "description": "Description of the work",
  "image": "ar://IMAGE_TX_ID",
  "content": {
    "mime": "image/jpeg",
    "uri": "ar://MEDIA_TX_ID"
  }
}
```

For video/audio, also set `"animation_url": "ar://MEDIA_TX_ID"`.

**Next.js API proxy route** (`app/api/mint/route.ts`):
```typescript
import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server";

const IN_PROCESS_API = "https://api.inprocess.world/api";

export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {
  const body = await req.json();
  const res = await fetch(`${IN_PROCESS_API}/moment/create`, {
    method: "POST",
    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    body: JSON.stringify(body),
  });
  const data = await res.json();
  return NextResponse.json(data);
}
```

**Request body sent from your client:**
```json
{
  "contract": {
    "address": "0xYOUR_COLLECTION_ADDRESS"
  },
  "token": {
    "tokenMetadataURI": "ar://METADATA_TX_ID",
    "createReferral": "0x749B7b7A6944d72266Be9500FC8C221B6A7554Ce",
    "salesConfig": {
      "type": "fixedPrice",
      "pricePerToken": "1000000000000000",
      "saleStart": "0",
      "saleEnd": "18446744073709551615"
    },
    "mintToCreatorCount": 1
  },
  "account": "0xARTIST_WALLET_ADDRESS"
}
```

- `pricePerToken` is in wei. `"1000000000000000"` = 0.001 ETH. Use `"0"` for free.
- `saleEnd: "18446744073709551615"` means no expiry (max uint64).
- `account` is the artist's wallet address — this determines who owns the token.
- `createReferral` is optional but recommended; use `NEXT_PUBLIC_CREATE_REFERRAL` from your env.

**Response:**
```json
{ "hash": "0x...", "chainId": 8453 }
```

Reference: https://docs.inprocess.world/api-reference/moment/create
Reference (sales config): https://docs.inprocess.world/configuration/sales-config
Reference (splits): https://docs.inprocess.world/configuration/splits

---

### Step 9: Display Artist Profile

**GET `/api/profile?address=0x...`** — public, no auth required.

```typescript
const res = await fetch(
  `https://api.inprocess.world/api/profile?address=${walletAddress}`
);
const profile = await res.json();
// profile.username, profile.bio, profile.avatar_url
// profile.twitter_username, profile.instagram_username, profile.farcaster_username
```

Reference: https://docs.inprocess.world/api-reference/artists

---

### Step 10: Revenue Splits (Optional)

To split proceeds between multiple wallets, add a `splits` array to the moment create request. Minimum 2 recipients, percentages must sum to 100.

```json
{
  "splits": [
    { "address": "0xARTIST_ADDRESS", "percentAllocation": 80 },
    { "address": "0xCOLLABORATOR_ADDRESS", "percentAllocation": 20 }
  ]
}
```

Reference: https://docs.inprocess.world/configuration/splits

---

### Minimal File Structure for a Custom Client

```
your-client/
├── app/
│   ├── page.tsx                  # Timeline / discover page
│   ├── mint/page.tsx             # Mint form page
│   ├── layout.tsx                # Root layout with providers
│   └── api/
│       ├── timeline/route.ts     # Proxy: GET /api/timeline
│       ├── collect/route.ts      # Proxy: POST /api/moment/collect
│       ├── mint/route.ts         # Proxy: POST /api/moment/create
│       └── upload/route.ts       # Upload media to Arweave
├── components/
│   ├── MomentCard.tsx            # Displays a single moment
│   ├── CollectButton.tsx         # Collect button with wallet check
│   ├── MintForm.tsx              # 3-step upload + mint form
│   └── Nav.tsx                   # Header with ConnectButton
├── lib/
│   ├── inprocess.ts              # Type definitions, helper functions
│   └── wagmi.ts                  # Wagmi + RainbowKit config
├── providers/
│   └── WagmiProvider.tsx         # Provider stack
└── .env.local                    # Environment variables
```

---

### Key Concepts Summary

| Concept | Detail |
|---|---|
| API base URL | `https://api.inprocess.world/api` |
| Authentication | `x-api-key: art_sk_...` header (server-side only) |
| Public endpoints | GET /timeline, GET /moment, GET /collections, GET /profile |
| Protected endpoints | POST /moment/collect, POST /moment/airdrop, POST /moment/hide |
| Create moment | POST /moment/create — no API key, but `account` (artist address) required |
| Blockchain | Base mainnet (chain ID: 8453) |
| Media storage | Arweave — returns permanent `ar://TX_ID` URIs |
| Metadata format | JSON with `name`, `description`, `image`, `content.mime`, `content.uri` |
| Collection | ERC-1155 contract — create once, mint many moments to it |
| Price unit | wei (1 ETH = 1e18 wei). Use `"0"` for free mints |

---

### Common Mistakes to Avoid

1. **Never expose `INPROCESS_API_KEY` in client-side code.** Always proxy through `/app/api/` routes.
2. **Upload media to Arweave before calling create.** The `tokenMetadataURI` must be an `ar://` URI pointing to already-uploaded metadata JSON.
3. **The `account` field in `/moment/create` must match an admin of the collection.** If you get a permission error, verify the wallet is an admin.
4. **`pricePerToken` is a string, not a number.** The API uses BigInt-as-string to avoid JavaScript precision loss.
5. **`chainId` is always `8453` for Base mainnet.** Use `84532` only for Base Sepolia testnet during development.
